text.title: Spatial Accessibility in XR Interfaces Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Spatial Accessibility in XR Interfaces methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Panels classify into near (0.3–0.6m), mid (0.6–1.2m), far (>1.2m). Critical actions MUST be reachable from at least one zone matching seated AND standing pose.

rationale.source: Microsoft MR design + Meta Quest design

rule.rationale: Out-of-reach controls force uncomfortable motion or exclude.

rule.statement: UI positioning MUST recalibrate at session start from user height (1.4m–2.0m range). Fixed Y positions are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Fixed heights exclude short / tall users.

rule.statement: ≤1 critical panel may occlude another at any time. Stacking critical panels is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Occluded info forces head motion / exclusion.

rule.statement: Critical events MUST emit spatial audio with direction matching the visual; mono notifications are forbidden for spatial UI.
rule.rationale: Blind / low-vision users rely on direction.

rule.statement: Standing-only locomotion / interactions MUST ship a seated alternate.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-spatial-accessibility.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
